heading: Special rules for credits and deductions
label: 642 Special rules for credits and deductions
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title
Editorial Notes

References in TextSection 68(b)(1)(C), referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(C)(i)(I), which defined applicable amount for an individual who is not married and who is not a surviving spouse or head of household, was omitted in the general amendment of by , , . The new subsec. (b) of does not contain any paragraphs and does not define applicable amount.section 68 of this titlePub. L. 119–21, title VII, § 70111(a)139 Stat. 164section 68 of this titleJuly 4, 2025

Amendments2018—Subsec. (c)(1). substituted “other than” for “other then”.Pub. L. 115–1412017—Subsec. (b)(2)(C)(iii). added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 115–972014—Subsec. (b)(2)(C)(i)(I). substituted “section 68(b)(1)(C)” for “section 151(d)(3)(C)(iii)”.Pub. L. 113–2952002—Subsec. (b). reenacted heading without change and amended text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “An estate shall be allowed a deduction of $600. A trust which, under its governing instrument, is required to distribute all of its income currently shall be allowed a deduction of $300. All other trusts shall be allowed a deduction of $100. The deductions allowed by this subsection shall be in lieu of the deductions allowed under section 151 (relating to deduction for personal exemption).”Pub. L. 107–1341996—Subsec. (g). substituted “under section 2621(a)(2)” for “under 2621(a)(2)”.Pub. L. 104–1881993—Subsec. (c)(4). , amended heading and text of par. (4) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In the case of a trust, the deduction allowed by this subsection shall be subject to section 681 (relating to unrelated business income).”Pub. children.heading: Foreign tax credit allowed
children.label: a Foreign tax credit allowed
children.text: An estate or trust shall be allowed the credit against tax for taxes imposed by foreign countries and possessions of the United States, to the extent allowed by section 901, only in respect of so much of the taxes described in such section as is not properly allocable under such section to the beneficiaries.

children.heading: Estates
children.label: 1 Estates
children.text: An estate shall be allowed a deduction of $600.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, a trust shall be allowed a deduction of $100.

children.heading: Trusts distributing income currently
children.label: B Trusts distributing income currently
children.text: A trust which, under its governing instrument, is required to distribute all of its income currently shall be allowed a deduction of $300.

children.label: I
children.text: by treating such trust as an individual described in section 68(b)(1)(C), and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: II
children.text: by applying section 67(e) (without the reference to section 642(b)) for purposes of determining the adjusted gross income of the trust.

children.label: I
children.text: such trust is a disability trust described in subsection (c)(2)(B)(iv) of section 1917 of the Social Security Act (), and42 U.S.C. 1396p

children.label: II
children.text: all of the beneficiaries of the trust as of the close of the taxable year are determined by the Commissioner of Social Security to have been disabled (within the meaning of section 1614(a)(3) of the Social Security Act, ) for some portion of such year.42 U.S.C. 1382c(a)(3)

children.heading: Years when personal exemption amount is zero
children.label: iii Years when personal exemption amount is zero

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In the case of any taxable year in which the exemption amount under section 151(d) is zero, clause (i) shall be applied by substituting “$4,150” for “the exemption amount under section 151(d)”.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: II Inflation adjustment
children.text: In the case of any taxable year beginning in a calendar year after 2018, the $4,150 amount in subparagraph (A) shall be increased in the same manner as provided in section 6334(d)(4)(C).

children.heading: Deductions in lieu of personal exemption
children.label: 3 Deductions in lieu of personal exemption
children.text: The deductions allowed by this subsection shall be in lieu of the deductions allowed under section 151 (relating to deduction for personal exemption).

children.heading: Deduction for amounts paid or permanently set aside for a charitable purpose
children.label: c Deduction for amounts paid or permanently set aside for a charitable purpose

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In the case of an estate or trust (other than a trust meeting the specifications of subpart B), there shall be allowed as a deduction in computing its taxable income (in lieu of the deduction allowed by section 170(a), relating to deduction for charitable, etc., contributions and gifts) any amount of the gross income, without limitation, which pursuant to the terms of the governing instrument is, during the taxable year, paid for a purpose specified in section 170(c) (determined without regard to section 170(c)(2)(A)). If a charitable contribution is paid after the close of such taxable year and on or before the last day of the year following the close of such taxable year, then the trustee or administrator may elect to treat such contribution as paid during such taxable year. The election shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary prescribes by regulations.

children.label: i
children.text: an irrevocable remainder interest is transferred to or for the use of an organization described in section 170(c), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the grantor is at all times after , under a mental disability to change the terms of the trust; orOctober 9, 1969

children.label: i
children.text: the testator dies before , without having republished the will after , by codicil or otherwise,October 9, 1972October 9, 1969

children.label: ii
children.text: the testator at no time after , had the right to change the portions of the will which pertain to the trust, orOctober 9, 1969

children.label: iii
children.text: the will is not republished by codicil or otherwise before , and the testator is on such date and at all times thereafter under a mental disability to republish the will by codicil or otherwise,October 9, 1972

children.heading: Pooled income funds
children.label: 3 Pooled income funds
children.text: In the case of a pooled income fund (as defined in paragraph (5)), there shall also be allowed as a deduction in computing its taxable income any amount of the gross income attributable to gain from the sale of a capital asset held for more than 1 year, without limitation, which pursuant to the terms of the governing instrument is, during the taxable year, permanently set aside for a purpose specified in section 170(c).

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: 4 Adjustments
children.text: To the extent that the amount otherwise allowable as a deduction under this subsection consists of gain described in section 1202(a), proper adjustment shall be made for any exclusion allowable to the estate or trust under section 1202. In the case of a trust, the deduction allowed by this subsection shall be subject to section 681 (relating to unrelated business income).

children.label: A
children.text: to which each donor transfers property, contributing an irrevocable remainder interest in such property to or for the use of an organization described in section 170(b)(1)(A) (other than in clauses (vii) or (viii)), and retaining an income interest for the life of one or more beneficiaries (living at the time of such transfer),

children.label: B
children.text: in which the property transferred by each donor is commingled with property transferred by other donors who have made or make similar transfers,

children.label: C
children.text: which cannot have investments in securities which are exempt from the taxes imposed by this subtitle,

children.label: D
children.text: which includes only amounts received from transfers which meet the requirements of this paragraph,

children.label: E
children.text: which is maintained by the organization to which the remainder interest is contributed and of which no donor or beneficiary of an income interest is a trustee, and

children.label: F
children.text: from which each beneficiary of an income interest receives income, for each year for which he is entitled to receive the income interest referred to in subparagraph (A), determined by the rate of return earned by the trust for such year.

children.heading: Taxable private foundations
children.label: 6 Taxable private foundations
children.text: In the case of a private foundation which is not exempt from taxation under section 501(a) for the taxable year, the provisions of this subsection shall not apply and the provisions of section 170 shall apply.

children.heading: Net operating loss deduction
children.label: d Net operating loss deduction
children.text: The benefit of the deduction for net operating losses provided by section 172 shall be allowed to estates and trusts under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Deduction for depreciation and depletion
children.label: e Deduction for depreciation and depletion
children.text: An estate or trust shall be allowed the deduction for depreciation and depletion only to the extent not allowable to beneficiaries under sections 167(d) and 611(b).

children.heading: Amortization deductions
children.label: f Amortization deductions
children.text: The benefit of the deductions for amortization provided by sections 169 and 197 shall be allowed to estates and trusts in the same manner as in the case of an individual. The allowable deduction shall be apportioned between the income beneficiaries and the fiduciary under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Disallowance of double deductions
children.label: g Disallowance of double deductions
children.text: Amounts allowable under section 2053 or 2054 as a deduction in computing the taxable estate of a decedent shall not be allowed as a deduction (or as an offset against the sales price of property in determining gain or loss) in computing the taxable income of the estate or of any other person, unless there is filed, within the time and in the manner and form prescribed by the Secretary, a statement that the amounts have not been allowed as deductions under section 2053 or 2054 and a waiver of the right to have such amounts allowed at any time as deductions under section 2053 or 2054. Rules similar to the rules of the preceding sentence shall apply to amounts which may be taken into account under section 2621(a)(2) or 2622(b). This subsection shall not apply with respect to deductions allowed under part II (relating to income in respect of decedents).

children.heading: Unused loss carryovers and excess deductions on termination available to beneficiaries
children.label: h Unused loss carryovers and excess deductions on termination available to beneficiaries

children.text: a net operating loss carryover under section 172 or a capital loss carryover under section 1212, or
for the last taxable year of the estate or trust deductions (other than the deductions allowed under subsections (b) or (c)) in excess of gross income for such year,

children.heading: Certain distributions by cemetery perpetual care funds
children.label: i Certain distributions by cemetery perpetual care funds

children.text: was created pursuant to local law by a taxable cemetery corporation for the care and maintenance of cemetery property, and
is treated for the taxable year as a trust for purposes of this subchapter,
